growth_records.medium: sand microcosm (4.8% gravimetric water content) contaminated with dibenzofuran (DBF)
growth_records.cite: Moreno-Forero et al. Genome-wide analysis of Sphingomonas wittichii RW1 behaviour during inoculation and growth in contaminated sand. The ISME Journal (2014). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2014.101. [PMC4274413]
growth_records.snippet: Population development of RW1 during the early phase in sand with DBF (12-28 h) was best represented by exponential growth ... with a calculated growth rate of 0.24±0.06 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate (0.24 +/- 0.06 h-1) directly reported in paper, calculated by the authors themselves from the increase of log CFU/g sand over time (12-28 h post-inoculation) in triplicate microcosms (Methods: 'The growth rate in soil was calculated from the increase of the log CFU over time'). doubling_time_h derived here as ln2/0.24 = 2.89 h for completeness. Incubation temperature not explicitly stated for the sand microcosm growth assay (sand was stored at room temperature 18-22C prior to use, but this is not confirmed as the incubation temperature during the growth experiment itself, so temperature_C left null).

growth_records.medium: DSM457 phosphate-buffered mineral medium with DBF crystals
growth_records.cite: Moreno-Forero et al. Genome-wide analysis of Sphingomonas wittichii RW1 behaviour during inoculation and growth in contaminated sand. The ISME Journal (2014). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2014.101. [PMC4274413]
growth_records.snippet: This is similar to the growth rate observed in liquid cultures with DBF crystals (0.23±0.01 h-1).
growth_records.notes: Growth rate (0.23 +/- 0.01 h-1) for RW1 growing in liquid batch culture on DBF crystals (DSM457 mineral medium, 30C, 180 rpm rotary shaking, aerobic) directly reported in the paper, determined by the same CFU-based method as the sand cultures. doubling_time_h derived here as ln2/0.23 = 3.01 h for completeness.
